message.content:
Dans le dépôt de code pour les exemples Terraform, et en particulier l'exemple sur Terraform module testing, on va améliorer un petit peu l'existant, qui est très bien. L'idée, ça serait de pouvoir faire la démo en plusieurs temps. Dans un premier temps, je vais présenter le module et la structure du répertoire. Dans un premier temps, ça peut être sympa, dans une première étape, de scinder le truc en étapes, dans une première étape de faire les tests les plus basiques, c'est les tests de validation. Puis ensuite, de faire potentiellement les tests rapides de MOOC, qui n'ont pas besoin d'authentification. Et enfin, d'aller sur un test d'intégration end-to-end. Ça serait top de se baser sur la pyramide de tests qui est abordée dans la pièce live associée, qui est la dernière pièce live. Et de faire le lien entre cette pyramide de tests et les différentes stages dans lesquelles on peut lancer ça en mode démo. L'idée, c'est que ce dépôt, là, il ne va pas être lancé one shot. Il y aura vraiment une démo et on va passer une bonne vingtaine de minutes dessus en expliquant comment ça marche, pourquoi on l'écrit, la première étape, les tests de validation. Ensuite, on va aller sur les plans. Ensuite, on va aller sur les plans. Ensuite, on va aller sur la parallélisation. Voilà, donc il y a pas mal de sujets. Propose-moi un plan pour améliorer un petit peu ce dépôt ainsi que son README pour que ça colle avec ce mode démo progressive.

Ensuite, en te basant sur la documentation, j'aimerais que tu vérifies qu'on n'est pas passé à côté de quelque chose d'intéressant ou d'important donc fais une exploration un peu précise de la doc pour être sûr que l'on ne passe pas à côté d'une belle feature qui serait sortie récemment avec par exemple la version 1.15 et donc de bien vérifier que l'on couvre les features on va dire principales et les features gold. voilà le golden pass des Terraform tests par rapport à ce qu'il est possible de faire dans la doc.

Ah, et dernier point, j'ai l'impression que le code généré dans cet exemple Terraform Adult Testing ne respecte pas du tout ce qui a été configuré en termes de convention de dev et de langues, donc vérifie que c'est cohérent puisque tous les autres exemples sont en anglais, donc corrige les éventuels codes ou commentaires qui pourraient rester en français, et fait une passe de vérification globale par rapport à ce qui est dans l'agent.md. S'il y a des choses qui manquent et des conventions qui sont simplement étendues de par les autres exemples, propose-moi ensuite un plan de mise à jour de l'agent.md de ce dépôt pour que ça soit cohérent. Donc un conventional commit, semantic release, éventuellement, je ne sais pas si on l'a mis dans la CI, le Lint, le process à mettre en place, pré-commit, les conventions de langue, les bonnes pratiques de dev, et voilà, tout ce que tu pourrais voir d'angle que je pourrais rater maintenant, n'hésite pas à l'ajouter pour le proposer.

Et dernière chose assez importante, vérifie la pièce live et l'idée associée pour voir si toutes les idées que nous avons eues justement dans la conception de cet élément éditorial sont bien abordées dans le dépôt d'exemples et que l'on n'a rien oublié par rapport à notre conception initiale. Je veux un tableau avec la liste des éléments qui ont été prévus et la liste des éléments qui sont effectivement présents dans le dépôt.


En termes de priorités, fais-moi une synthèse des tâches et propose-moi rapidement une petite question en direct dans le chat pour sélectionner la P1, la P2, la P3.

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. ## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

attachment.content:
- handoff: End-of-session handoff. Reconcile the project's tracking file (mark done resolved, promote every leftover to a live item with value+trigger, log decisions/debt/lessons), verify green via the project's oracle command, confirm nothing lives only in chat or a disposable note, persist a disposable resume prompt (with a cold-start reload list of the links that mattered this session) into the tracking file, checkpoint-commit, then compact the note. Project-agnostic. Use when wrapping up, pausing, "prep handoff", "close the session", or "generate a resume prompt".
- linkedin-review: Multi-agent parallel review of a LinkedIn profile against benchmarks, via Playwright MCP browser navigation. Spawns N specialized personas (decideur-client, commercial-esn, personal-branding, concurrent-freelance, copywriter, ats-keywords, seo-linkedin) in parallel, each producing an independent critique. Returns a synthesized report with convergences, conflicts, prioritized actions. Use when the user asks to audit/review their LinkedIn profile.
- review-plan: Multi-agent parallel review of a plan, design doc, or architecture proposal. Spawns N expert personas (product, security, performance, seo, devex, cloud-architecture, finops, compliance, accessibility) in parallel, each producing an independent critique. Returns a synthesized report with convergences, conflicts, and decisions to make. Use when the user asks to review a plan, audit a design, get multiple perspectives on a doc, or run a "multi-agent review".
- rtk: RTK (Rust Token Killer) proxy meta-commands. Use when the user asks for RTK token-savings analytics, command history, missed-opportunity discovery, or wants to bypass the auto-rewrite hook (rtk proxy). The PreToolUse hook auto-rewrites Bash commands transparently - do not invoke this skill for normal commands.
- add-contact: Create one CRM Contact via add-contact.py (deterministic, schema-validated). Use when adding a person with optional Org affiliations. For multi-entity free text, use add-lead.
- add-editorial-idea: Capture a new editorial idea (LinkedIn post, article, talk seed) as an anchor in `02-Areas/Éditorial/Ideas/`. Runs dedup check, applies frontmatter schema, enforces BGA brand conformity, updates MoC. Auto-detects express vs standard mode. Use when the user wants to capture, add, save, or register an editorial idea, post idea, article idea, or content seed.
- add-editorial-piece: Transform an editorial anchor (`Ideas/<file>.md`) into a piece for a given format (linkedin, article, live, podcast, talk). Resolves the anchor, fills frontmatter from template SSoT, drafts the body, runs brand conformity, ticks the matching line in the anchor's Déclinaisons checklist. Use when the user wants to draft a LinkedIn post, article, talk, live, or podcast piece from an existing idea.
- add-editorial-slides: Generate a branded HTML slide deck for a piece from the BGA slides skeleton. Composes slides from the piece narrative (Why/How/What, key points, tables) using slide-type primitives (title/bullets/cards/table/flow), renders each slide for a legibility gate, runs brand-review, wires the deck back into the piece. Interactive HTML opened in the browser, no delivery PNG. Use when the user wants intro slides, a live deck, or a talk/workshop deck for an editorial piece.
- add-editorial-visual: Generate a branded editorial visual (square post visual or LinkedIn event banner) for a piece from the BGA HTML skeleton. Reads the piece's `## Visuels` brief (format + disposition + setup/Why + contenu/How + synthèse/What), composes with CSS auto-layout (never hand-placed coordinates), renders HTML->PNG via Playwright, gates on visual legibility + brand-review, wires the asset back into the piece. Use when the user wants a schema, diagram, before/after, lifecycle visual, or event banner for a LinkedIn post, article, or live.
- add-formation: Bootstrap a new BGA formation by aggregating N editorial anchors (`Ideas/*.md`) into a curriculum. Creates the formation folder, generates the index from template, scaffolds one module file per anchor, backlinks each anchor via its Déclinaisons checklist, updates the Formations MoC. Supports direct mode (--ideas arg) or interactive mode (anchor picker grouped by pillar). Use when the user wants to create, scaffold, build, or initialize a training, workshop, masterclass, or curriculum.
- add-interaction: Create one CRM Interaction (dated exchange report) via add-interaction.py (deterministic, schema-validated). Use to log a call/meeting/email/event tied to an Opp or Org.
- add-lead: Add a new prospect (Organisation + optional Opportunity + optional Contact) to the CRM. Accepts free text and/or LinkedIn URLs. Use when the user mentions a new company or contact to add.
- add-opportunity: Create one CRM Opportunity via add-opp.py (deterministic, schema-validated). Use when adding a new channel/deal on an existing or new target Org. One Opp = one channel.
- add-organisation: Create one CRM Organisation via add-org.py (deterministic, schema-validated). Use when adding a single company (no opportunity/contact). For multi-entity free text + LinkedIn, use add-lead.
- archive-opportunity: Move a closed CRM Opportunity (perdu/gagne) to Opportunities/_archive/ via archive-opp.py (git mv, FK-safe). Use to declutter active pipeline after a deal closes.
- archive-project: Guided PARA archival of a finished project. Extracts forward-looking artefacts BEFORE the move - active backlog to Projects, living doc/SSoT to an existing or newly-created Area, structural decisions to ADRs, pure reference to Resources - sets the frozen marker, git mv to 04-Archives, propagates orphan wikilinks. Judgment-heavy (classify + route); writes = git mv + targeted edits. Use when a project is done and must be archived.
- brand-review: Audit a BGA editorial artifact (text and/or visual) against brand SSoT. Verifies tone-of-voice principles, format specs, color tokens, WCAG ratios, text/visual coherence. Outputs a status table + one prioritized recommendation.
- parse-td-results: Parse a Tutorial Dojo HTML exam result, extract incorrect questions, and append summaries to the AWS SAP Mistakes note.
- synthesize-interaction: Synthesize a freelance prospection Interaction draft - restructure the raw note and propagate to the linked Opportunity/Organisation. Thick orchestrator - LLM synthesis only, writes delegated to deterministic scripts.
- update-contact: Update an existing CRM Contact via update-contact.py (deterministic, schema-validated, idempotent). Set personal fields, append Org affiliations or notes.
- update-lead: Quick update of an existing Organisation or Opportunity from free text (informal conversation, phone call, ad-hoc info). Auto-detects the target. Use when the user reports info about an existing prospect.
- update-opportunity: Update an existing CRM Opportunity (non-status fields) via update-opp.py (deterministic, schema-validated, idempotent). For status/lost/won transitions use transition-opp.
- update-organisation: Update an existing CRM Organisation via update-org.py (deterministic, schema-validated, idempotent). Set durable company fields or append to body regions.
- weekly-actions: Display prospection actions for the current calendar week (Mon-Sun), including overdue items. Source - Opportunities. Use when the user asks for actions, planning, or prospection to-do.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

name: BGA editorial - tournure positive par défaut
description: Préférence éditoriale BGA Consulting - formuler en affirmatif, éviter "pas de", "ne plus", "sans", négations multiples. Codifié en principe 2.7 du tone of voice.
type: feedback
originSessionId: af299ac6-6342-4e28-a091-36390e0d8955
---
Toute production éditoriale BGA Consulting (posts LinkedIn, articles, mails, slides, docs client) doit privilégier les tournures affirmatives. Éviter "pas de X", "ne plus", "sans", négations multiples. Dire ce qu'on fait / protège / rend possible plutôt que ce qu'on évite.

**Why** : marque posée et exigeante, pas alarmiste. Une marque conseil rassure par ce qu'elle construit, pas par ce qu'elle bannit. Le négatif fatigue à la lecture longue et signale une posture défensive. Demande explicite Baptiste 2026-05-10.

**How to apply** :
- Reformuler systématiquement les phrases négatives en affirmatif lors de toute rédaction ou relecture BGA.
- Exception assumée : listes Do/Don't, anti-positions du brief, règles bloquantes (WCAG FAIL, em-dash interdit, etc.) - le négatif y joue un rôle de frontière.
- Codifié dans `02-Areas/BGA Consulting/Brand/05-Tone-of-voice.md` section 2.7.
- Lors d'une passe validation sur un texte BGA, traiter ce critère au même titre que les coquilles et le fact-check.

# terraform-module-testing

> **type** `lab` · **tags** `aws` `terraform-test` `testing` `s3` `validation` `mock` `parallel`

Tester un module Terraform avec le framework natif `terraform test` : valider les entrées, le casser exprès, le déployer pour de vrai. Support du live "Terraform test" (2026-06-25).

## Le module

`modules/s3-bucket/` crée un bucket S3 avec versioning, blocage des accès publics et chiffrement SSE-KMS conditionnel. Il porte des garde-fous :

- **validations simples** : nom de bucket (règles S3), `environment` (dev/staging/prod) ;
- **validations croisées** (Terraform 1.9+) : `enable_encryption` exige `kms_key_arn` ; `prod` interdit `force_destroy` ;
- **chiffrement conditionnel** : `count = var.kms_key_arn != null ? 1 : 0` (la décision se prend sur la valeur de l'ARN).

## Les tests

| Fichier | command | Cloud | Couvre |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tests/validations.tftest.hcl` | plan | non (mock) | `expect_failures` sur chaque validateur |
| `tests/plan.tftest.hcl` | plan | non (mock) | toutes les variables câblées dans le plan |
| `tests/deploy.tftest.hcl` | apply | oui (sandbox) | déploiement réel + assertions ressources |
| `tests/parallel.tftest.hcl` | apply | oui (sandbox) | `parallel` + `state_key`, 2 déploiements concurrents |

Les deux premières couches tournent **sans credentials** (CI sans secret). Les deux dernières déploient sur un vrai compte (`AWS_PROFILE=sandbox`) et `terraform test` détruit en fin de fichier.

## Lancer

```bash
make init
make test-fast                  # validations + plan mocké (creds-free)
AWS_PROFILE=sandbox make test    # suite complète (apply réel + auto-destroy)
```

## Nettoyage sur crash

Si `terraform test` est interrompu avant son auto-destroy, l'état en mémoire disparaît et les ressources fuient. Chaque ressource porte un tag de suite (`default_tags`), donc un sweeper les retrouve :

```bash
./sweep.sh            # liste les buckets taggés (dry-run)
./sweep.sh --force    # vide + supprime
```

Le natif `skip_cleanup` + `terraform test cleanup` arrive en preview Terraform 1.16.

# AGENTS.md

Guidance for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, OpenAI Codex, Aider, etc.) working in this repository. A `CLAUDE.md` symlink points here so Claude Code picks it up automatically. Each example also has its own nested `AGENTS.md` with example-specific guidance - read it before editing inside an example.

## What this repo is

A **library of self-contained Terraform examples**. Each lives under `examples/<name>/` and is independent: its own `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, tooling (`.terraform-version`, `.tflint.hcl`) and Terraform root module(s). Examples are **not** factored into shared code - isolation is intentional so each can be read, run, and live-demoed on its own.

Use cases served: reusable patterns, illustrations for blog/LinkedIn posts, and starting points / labs for live tech sessions.

## Taxonomy

Every example declares a primary `type` plus free-form tags. Source of truth is the catalogue table in the root `README.md`; each example's `README.md`/`AGENTS.md` restates its type.

- `pattern` - reusable, production-leaning reference.
- `lab` - starting point / TP with progressive steps for a live session.
- `experiment` - preview/RC features, may be intentionally unstable.

Do **not** create top-level category directories (`patterns/`, `experiments/`...). Keep `examples/` flat and classify via `type` + tags. This avoids lifecycle churn when an example changes category.

## How to add an example (golden path)

1. `mkdir examples/<name>/` - kebab-case, scope-first (`aws-multi-env`, `terraform-rc-variables`).
2. Add `README.md` (state its `type` + tags, objective, run steps) and `AGENTS.md` (example-specific guidance).
3. Add per-example tooling: `.terraform-version` (pin, may be an RC/prerelease for `experiment` types) and `.tflint.hcl`.
4. Add the Terraform root module(s). Any directory containing a `providers.tf` is auto-discovered by CI - no CI edit needed to validate it.
5. Register a row in the root `README.md` catalogue table.

## CI

`.github/workflows/terraform.yml` auto-discovers Terraform root modules (directories containing `providers.tf`) under `examples/` and runs `validate` + `tflint` + `trivy` per module in a matrix. `terraform fmt -check -recursive` runs once at the root. The `tf-setup` composite action installs Terraform from the target example's `.terraform-version`. Adding an example requires no CI change.

## Repository standards

- **Git flow**: GitHub flow (short-lived feature branches off `main`, PR review, squash-merge).
- **Work tracking**: durable backlog = GitHub Issues + Milestone (a goal + its acceptance criteria); each PR references its issue (`Closes #N`). `main` carries no task file - session plans stay local/ephemeral. `tasks/lessons.md` holds durable repo lessons.
- **Commit messages**: [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) - `<type>(<scope>): <subject>`. Use the example name as scope when relevant (e.g. `feat(aws-multi-env): ...`).
  - Common types: `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `chore`, `refactor`, `test`, `ci`, `build`.
  - Breaking changes: append `!` after type/scope or add a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer.
  - Keep subject imperative, lowercase, no trailing period, ~72 chars max.
- **Versioning**: [SemVer](https://semver.org/), repo-level single release line via release-please.
- **Validation before commit**: `terraform fmt -recursive` (root) and `terraform validate` inside each touched root module.

A library of self-contained Terraform examples: reusable patterns, illustrations for blog/LinkedIn posts, and starting points for live tech sessions ("Matinale Tech").

Each example lives under [`examples/`](examples/) and is fully independent - its own `README.md`, tooling (`.terraform-version`, `.tflint.hcl`) and Terraform code. Pick one, `cd` into it, follow its README.

| [`terraform-deferred-actions`](examples/terraform-deferred-actions/) | `experiment` | `rc` `deferred-actions` `kms` `unknown-at-plan` | Testing Terraform's experimental *deferred actions* (`plan -allow-deferral`) to inject a KMS key whose ARN is unknown at plan time. |

| [`terraform-module-testing`](examples/terraform-module-testing/) | `lab` | `aws` `terraform-test` `testing` `s3` `validation` | Test a reusable S3 module with the native `terraform test` framework: variable validations (`expect_failures`), plan assertions with `mock_provider`, real apply on AWS, and parallel runs with `state_key`. |

# Demo run-of-show - live "Terraform test" (2026-06-25).
# terraform 1.15.6 (cf .terraform-version). L'apply réel cible le profil AWS sandbox.
TF := terraform

.PHONY: init fmt validate test test-fast test-deploy test-parallel sweep clean

init:          ## init (providers : aws + random)
	$(TF) init

fmt:           ## format check
	$(TF) fmt -recursive -check -diff

validate:      ## validate la config
	$(TF) validate

test-fast:     ## couche creds-free : validations + plan mocké (tourne en CI sans secret)
	$(TF) test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl -verbose

test-deploy:   ## integration : apply réel sur AWS (AWS_PROFILE=sandbox requis)
	AWS_PROFILE=sandbox $(TF) test -filter=tests/deploy.tftest.hcl

test-parallel: ## parallel + state_key : 2 déploiements concurrents (AWS_PROFILE=sandbox)
	AWS_PROFILE=sandbox $(TF) test -filter=tests/parallel.tftest.hcl

test:          ## suite complète (AWS_PROFILE=sandbox pour les couches apply)
	AWS_PROFILE=sandbox $(TF) test

sweep:         ## nettoyage des ressources orphelines par tag (cf sweep.sh)
	./sweep.sh

clean:         ## supprime l'état local terraform
	find . -type d -name '.terraform' -prune -exec rm -rf {} + ; find . -name '*.tfstate*' -delete

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Sweeper : nettoie les buckets de test orphelins (laissés par un `terraform test`
# interrompu avant son auto-destroy). Cible les ressources portant le tag de suite.
# Source de vérité du tag : providers.tf (default_tags tftest-suite).
#
# Usage:
#   ./sweep.sh           # liste les buckets taggés (dry-run)
#   ./sweep.sh --force   # vide puis supprime chaque bucket taggé
#
# Requiert AWS_PROFILE (ex: sandbox). Alternative outillée : aws-nuke / awsweeper
# filtrés sur le même tag.
set -euo pipefail

TAG_KEY="tftest-suite"
TAG_VALUE="terraform-module-testing"
REGION="${AWS_REGION:-eu-west-1}"
FORCE="${1:-}"

mapfile -t ARNS < <(aws resourcegroupstaggingapi get-resources \
  --region "$REGION" \
  --tag-filters "Key=${TAG_KEY},Values=${TAG_VALUE}" \
  --resource-type-filters "s3" \
  --query 'ResourceTagMappingList[].ResourceARN' --output text | tr '\t' '\n')

if [ "${#ARNS[@]}" -eq 0 ] || [ -z "${ARNS[0]:-}" ]; then
  echo "Aucun bucket taggé ${TAG_KEY}=${TAG_VALUE}."
  exit 0
fi

echo "Buckets taggés trouvés :"
printf '  %s\n' "${ARNS[@]}"

if [ "$FORCE" != "--force" ]; then
  echo "Dry-run. Relancer avec --force pour vider + supprimer."
  exit 0
fi

for arn in "${ARNS[@]}"; do
  bucket="${arn##*:::}"
  echo "Suppression de ${bucket} ..."
  aws s3 rm "s3://${bucket}" --recursive >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  aws s3api delete-bucket --bucket "${bucket}" --region "$REGION"
done
echo "Nettoyage terminé."

### 4. FEATURES À DÉMONTRER (FIL ROUGE ANNONCÉ)

**Source** : Pièce Live, section "Features à démontrer (fil rouge)"

Chaque item identifié par :
- **I** = slide intro
- **L** = live-codé
- **S** = slide/discussion

#### **I1** : Test IaC – c'est quoi, pourquoi (valider child + root module)
*Slide intro, ~1-2 min dans le segment 0 ou 1*
- Concepts : validation en child vs root
- Référence prévue : paysage des outils

#### **I2** : Place dans la pyramide → diamant (plan+mock ≈ unit-ish, apply = intégration, e2e/polling = hors scope natif)
*Slide intro*
- Pyramide/diamant des couches de test
- Démarcation : plan + mock ≈ couche unit (créds-less, rapide) vs apply = couche intégration vs e2e/polling = hors portée natif (Terratest)

#### **I3** : Paysage outils et « pourquoi maintenant » (OpenTofu, rachat IBM)
*Slide intro*
- OpenTofu août 2023
- terraform test GA 1.6 oct 2023
- Rachat IBM signalé comme contexte

#### **I4** : Comparatif feature × when-to-use (terraform test vs Terratest vs terraform-compliance + couche statique)
*Slide intro, tableau comparatif attendu*
- terraform test : HCL, validation/plan/apply, défaut dès le module
- Terratest : Go, e2e, polling, SDK ; preuve réelle
- terraform-compliance : BDD, policy sur le plan, conformité lisible
- tflint/trivy/OPA : lint, sécurité, policy, créds-less

#### **L1** : Module S3 (child) + exemple d'utilisation (root)
*Live-coding, segment 2, ouverture*
- Ouvrir `modules/s3-bucket/` : le module encode des décisions
- Montrer les règles encodées (nom, clé KMS, force_destroy interdit en prod)
- Reprise du module S3 + KMS du dernier live

#### **L2** : Validation simple (nom de bucket S3) + `expect_failures` (rouge → vert)
*Live-coding, segment 2*
- Montrer `variables.tf` : regex S3 pour nom, enum pour environment
- Montrer `tests/validations.tftest.hcl`
- **Anatomie d'un `.tftest.hcl`** : blocs `run`, `command = plan`, `module { source = "./modules/s3-bucket" }`, `expect_failures`
- Un run atomique par validateur (tip : message d'erreur isolé)
- `make test-fast` ou `terraform test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl` → **5 verts, sans AWS_PROFILE** (créds-less)
- **Démo rouge → vert** : commenter la validation croisée dans `variables.tf` (`enable_encryption`), relancer → `encryption_requires_kms_key` échoue (l'`expect_failures` n'est plus satisfait), restaurer

#### **L3** : Validation **croisée** (1.9+) : chiffrement ⇒ ARN KMS ; prod ⇒ pas de `force_destroy` + `expect_failures`
*Live-coding, segment 2*
- Validations croisées encodées dans `variables.tf` (Terraform 1.9+)
- Condition 1 : `!var.enable_encryption || var.kms_key_arn != null` → "kms_key_arn is required when enable_encryption is true."
- Condition 2 : `!(var.environment == "prod" && var.force_destroy)` → "force_destroy must be false when environment is prod."
- Tests : `encryption_requires_kms_key` et `prod_forbids_force_destroy` runs

#### **L4** : KMS conditionnel `count = var.kms_key_arn != null ? 1 : 0` (continuité du live deferred actions)
*Live-coding, segment 2*
- Montrer le locals : `kms_enabled = var.kms_key_arn != null`
- Ressource `aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration` piée sur `count = local.kms_enabled ? 1 : 0`
- Lien explicite : continuité du live deferred actions (même pattern de décision)

#### **L5** : Test de **plan**, toutes variables, `mock_provider` (créds-less, rapide)
*Live-coding, segment 3*
- Montrer `tests/plan.tftest.hcl`
- `mock_provider "aws" {}` + bloc `variables {}` au niveau fichier (tip live : pose des valeurs pour TOUS les run du fichier)
- Assertions sur la config planifiée :
  - Versioning `Enabled`
  - 4 flags public access block vrais
  - Bloc chiffrement présent/absent + propagation de l'ARN KMS
- `terraform test -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl` → vert en quelques secondes, **toujours sans credentials**
- Assertions détaillées :
  - `run "versioning_enabled_by_default"` : assert versioning status == "Enabled"
  - `run "public_access_fully_blocked"` : assert 4 flags = true
  - `run "no_encryption_block_without_key"` : assert length(config) == 0 si pas de KMS
  - `run "encryption_block_present_with_key"` : assert length(config) == 1 et ARN propagé

#### **L6** : **Apply réel** AWS sandbox + assertions ressources (versioning, SSE+KMS, public access block) + auto-destroy
*Live-coding, segment 3*
- `tests/deploy.tftest.hcl`
- `command = apply` sur un vrai compte AWS (`AWS_PROFILE=sandbox`)
- Run `setup` (suffixe aléatoire → nom globalement unique)
- Assertions sur les outputs réels :
  - `output.bucket_id` == nom demandé
  - `output.versioning_status` == "Enabled"
  - `output.encryption_enabled` == false (si pas de clé)
- **Auto-destroy** en fin de fichier (le pas de tir réel)
- Région cible `eu-west-1` (SCP region-lock organisationnelle, bon garde-fou à montrer)

#### **L7** : `parallel` + `state_key` : déployer l'exemple 2× sans collision
*Live-coding, segment 4*
- `tests/parallel.tftest.hcl`
- `parallel = true` + `state_key` distinct (`a`/`b`) → même exemple déployé 2×, états isolés
- Zéro collision de noms (suffixes différents via run.setup.suffix)
- `AWS_PROFILE=sandbox make test-parallel` → 2 buckets concurrents

#### **S1** : CI gate : exit-code, la couche mock tourne sans secrets
*Slide/discussion, segment 4*
- `terraform test` renvoie exit-code ; la couche `validations` + `plan` (mockée) tourne en CI **sans secret**
- Garde-fou avant merge

#### **S2** : Nettoyage sur crash : `default_tags` + sweeper (Resource Groups Tagging API / aws-nuke) ; teaser natif 1.16 `skip_cleanup`
*Slide/discussion, segment 4*
- State en mémoire, crash → buckets orphelins
- `default_tags` (providers.tf) pose un tag de run unique → `sweep.sh` les retrouve
- Resource Groups Tagging API (`aws resourcegroupstaggingapi get-resources --tag-filters`)
- Anecdote : SCP a forcé `eu-west-1` (garde-fou org réel)
- Teaser : `skip_cleanup` + `terraform test cleanup` en preview 1.16

#### **S3** : Reco layering (statique → terraform test → Terratest si e2e) + CTA
*Slide, segment 5*
- Layering : statique (tflint/trivy) → `terraform test` (unit mock + intégration) → Terratest si e2e/polling
- Repo public à l'écran (`examples/terraform-module-testing/`), prochaine étape, Calendly

### 5. TIPS ET PIÈGES (À COUVRIR EN LIVE)

**Source** : Pièce Live, section "Tips et pièges (à couvrir)"

#### **`variables {}` au niveau fichier**
- Pose des valeurs pour TOUS les `run {}` du fichier
- Chaque run surcharge au besoin
- Moins de répétition

#### **Runs unitaires pour les validations**
- Un validateur = un `run` (plan-only)
- Relecture simple, coût quasi nul
- Message d'erreur isolé

#### **Variables simples plutôt qu'objets complexes (retex)**
- `object({...})` rend le module visuellement propre mais complique l'usage et l'isolation du message d'erreur
- Pour ce qui doit être validé, préférer variables mono-type
- Un `validation` par attribut (plusieurs blocs par variable depuis 1.9)
- Continuité : live #1 portait `kms = object({arn, provided})` ; ici on aplatit pour la testabilité

#### **Wishlist HashiCorp (Fact-check Wave 1)**
- Pour une structure complexe, exposer l'attribut qui a déclenché l'erreur de validation
- Semble absent en 1.15.6, à confirmer sur le changelog 1.15/1.16

### 11. IMPLÉMENTATION RÉELLE DANS LE REPO (À COMPARER)

**Commit** : `f67c7e4 feat(terraform-module-testing): add native terraform test example`

#### Fichiers et structure implémentée

```
examples/terraform-module-testing/
├── .terraform-version          → 1.15.6
├── .terraform.lock.hcl
├── .tflint.hcl
├── Makefile                     → cibles test-fast, test-deploy, test-parallel, test
├── README.md                    → lien vers doc officielle, list des couches
├── AGENTS.md
├── providers.tf                 → default_tags avec tags tftest-suite/tftest-run
├── variables.tf                 → region, bucket_name, environment, enable_encryption, kms_key_arn, force_destroy, versioning_enabled, run_id
├── main.tf                      → module.bucket (root example)
├── outputs.tf                   → bucket_id, bucket_arn, versioning_status, encryption_enabled, kms_key_arn
├── sweep.sh                     → sweeper avec Resource Groups Tagging API
├── modules/s3-bucket/
│   ├── main.tf                  → bucket + public_access_block + versioning + SSE-KMS (count piloté sur kms_enabled)
│   ├── variables.tf             → bucket_name (validations regex), environment (enum), enable_encryption, kms_key_arn, force_destroy, versioning_enabled, tags
│   ├── outputs.tf
│   └── versions.tf              → Terraform >= 1.9.0, aws >= 5.0
└── tests/
    ├── setup/
    │   └── main.tf              → random_id pour suffixe unique
    ├── validations.tftest.hcl    → 5 runs avec expect_failures (bucket_name, environment, encryption_requires_kms_key, prod_forbids_force_destroy)
    ├── plan.tftest.hcl           → 4 runs avec mock_provider, assertions sur versioning/public-access/encryption
    ├── deploy.tftest.hcl         → 1 run apply réel (setup + deploy_and_assert)
    └── parallel.tftest.hcl       → 2 runs apply parallèles avec state_key distincts
```

#### Validations codées dans le module

**Dans `modules/s3-bucket/variables.tf`** :

1. **bucket_name** (L5-13) :
   - Longueur 3-63 caractères
   - Regex : `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9.-]*[a-z0-9]$`

2. **environment** (L16-24) :
   - Enum : `["dev", "staging", "prod"]`

3. **enable_encryption + kms_key_arn (croisée, L26-36)** :
   - `!var.enable_encryption || var.kms_key_arn != null`
   - Message : "kms_key_arn is required when enable_encryption is true."

4. **force_destroy + environment (croisée, L44-54)** :
   - `!(var.environment == "prod" && var.force_destroy)`
   - Message : "force_destroy must be false when environment is prod."

#### Tests implémentés

**`tests/validations.tftest.hcl`** (L1-70) :
- 5 runs atomiques avec `expect_failures` :
  - `bucket_name_too_short` : "ab" → expect fail on var.bucket_name
  - `bucket_name_invalid_chars` : "Mon_Bucket" → expect fail on var.bucket_name
  - `invalid_environment` : "production" → expect fail on var.environment
  - `encryption_requires_kms_key` : enable_encryption=true, kms_key_arn=null → expect fail on var.enable_encryption
  - `prod_forbids_force_destroy` : environment="prod", force_destroy=true → expect fail on var.force_destroy

**`tests/plan.tftest.hcl`** (L1-72) :
- `variables {}` au niveau fichier : bucket_name="demo-plan-bucket", environment="dev"
- 4 runs avec `mock_provider`, assertions sur config planifiée :
  - `versioning_enabled_by_default` : assert status == "Enabled"
  - `public_access_fully_blocked` : assert 4 flags = true
  - `no_encryption_block_without_key` : assert length(config) == 0
  - `encryption_block_present_with_key` : assert length(config) == 1 + ARN propagé

**`tests/deploy.tftest.hcl`** (L1-36) :
- Run `setup` : produit random_id.suffix (hex)
- Run `deploy_and_assert` : command=apply, variables bucket_name="bga-tftest-${run.setup.suffix}", assertions :
  - output.bucket_id == nom demandé
  - output.versioning_status == "Enabled"
  - output.encryption_enabled == false

**`tests/parallel.tftest.hcl`** (L1-42) :
- Run `setup` : random_id.suffix
- 2 runs apply parallèles :
  - `deploy_a` : parallel=true, state_key="a", bucket_name="bga-tftest-a-${run.setup.suffix}"
  - `deploy_b` : parallel=true, state_key="b", bucket_name="bga-tftest-b-${run.setup.suffix}"

#### Makefile targets

```makefile
make init              # terraform init (aws + random)
make fmt               # format check
make validate          # validate
make test-fast         # validations + plan (creds-free)
make test-deploy       # apply réel + assertions (AWS_PROFILE=sandbox)
make test-parallel     # parallel + state_key (AWS_PROFILE=sandbox)
make test              # suite complète
make sweep             # sweeper orphelins
make clean             # supprime .terraform + .tfstate*
```

#### Sweeper (sweep.sh)

- Cible Resource Groups Tagging API
- Tag-filter : `tftest-suite=terraform-module-testing`
- Usage : `./sweep.sh` (dry-run) ou `./sweep.sh --force` (exécute)

*Slide intro, tableau comparatif attendu*

#### **L1** : Module S3 (child) + exemple d'utilisation (root)
*Live-coding, segment 2, ouverture*
- Ouvrir `modules/s3-bucket/` : le module encode des décisions
- Montrer les règles encodées (nom, clé KMS, force_destroy interdit en prod)
- Reprise du module S3 + KMS du dernier live

#### **L2** : Validation simple (nom de bucket S3) + `expect_failures` (rouge → vert)
*Live-coding, segment 2*
- Montrer `variables.tf` : regex S3 pour nom, enum pour environment
- Montrer `tests/validations.tftest.hcl`
- **Anatomie d'un `.tftest.hcl`** : blocs `run`, `command = plan`, `module { source = "./modules/s3-bucket" }`, `expect_failures`
- Un run atomique par validateur (tip : message d'erreur isolé)
- `make test-fast` ou `terraform test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl` → **5 verts, sans AWS_PROFILE** (créds-less)
- **Démo rouge → vert** : commenter la validation croisée dans `variables.tf` (`enable_encryption`), relancer → `encryption_requires_kms_key` échoue (l'`expect_failures` n'est plus satisfait), restaurer

#### **L3** : Validation **croisée** (1.9+) : chiffrement ⇒ ARN KMS ; prod ⇒ pas de `force_destroy` + `expect_failures`
*Live-coding, segment 2*
- Validations croisées encodées dans `variables.tf` (Terraform 1.9+)
- Condition 1 : `!var.enable_encryption || var.kms_key_arn != null` → "kms_key_arn is required when enable_encryption is true."
- Condition 2 : `!(var.environment == "prod" && var.force_destroy)` → "force_destroy must be false when environment is prod."
- Tests : `encryption_requires_kms_key` et `prod_forbids_force_destroy` runs

#### **L4** : KMS conditionnel `count = var.kms_key_arn != null ? 1 : 0` (continuité du live deferred actions)
*Live-coding, segment 2*
- Montrer le locals : `kms_enabled = var.kms_key_arn != null`
- Ressource `aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration` piée sur `count = local.kms_enabled ? 1 : 0`
- Lien explicite : continuité du live deferred actions (même pattern de décision)

#### **L5** : Test de **plan**, toutes variables, `mock_provider` (créds-less, rapide)
*Live-coding, segment 3*
- Montrer `tests/plan.tftest.hcl`
- `mock_provider "aws" {}` + bloc `variables {}` au niveau fichier (tip live : pose des valeurs pour TOUS les run du fichier)
- Assertions sur la config planifiée :
  - Versioning `Enabled`
  - 4 flags public access block vrais
  - Bloc chiffrement présent/absent + propagation de l'ARN KMS
- `terraform test -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl` → vert en quelques secondes, **toujours sans credentials**
- Assertions détaillées :
  - `run "versioning_enabled_by_default"` : assert versioning status == "Enabled"
  - `run "public_access_fully_blocked"` : assert 4 flags = true
  - `run "no_encryption_block_without_key"` : assert length(config) == 0 si pas de KMS
  - `run "encryption_block_present_with_key"` : assert length(config) == 1 et ARN propagé

#### **L6** : **Apply réel** AWS sandbox + assertions ressources (versioning, SSE+KMS, public access block) + auto-destroy
*Live-coding, segment 3*
- `tests/deploy.tftest.hcl`
- `command = apply` sur un vrai compte AWS (`AWS_PROFILE=sandbox`)
- Run `setup` (suffixe aléatoire → nom globalement unique)
- Assertions sur les outputs réels :
  - `output.bucket_id` == nom demandé
  - `output.versioning_status` == "Enabled"
  - `output.encryption_enabled` == false (si pas de clé)
- **Auto-destroy** en fin de fichier (le pas de tir réel)
- Région cible `eu-west-1` (SCP region-lock organisationnelle, bon garde-fou à montrer)

#### **L7** : `parallel` + `state_key` : déployer l'exemple 2× sans collision
*Live-coding, segment 4*
- `tests/parallel.tftest.hcl`
- `parallel = true` + `state_key` distinct (`a`/`b`) → même exemple déployé 2×, états isolés
- Zéro collision de noms (suffixes différents via run.setup.suffix)
- `AWS_PROFILE=sandbox make test-parallel` → 2 buckets concurrents

#### **S1** : CI gate : exit-code, la couche mock tourne sans secrets
*Slide/discussion, segment 4*
- `terraform test` renvoie exit-code ; la couche `validations` + `plan` (mockée) tourne en CI **sans secret**
- Garde-fou avant merge

#### **S2** : Nettoyage sur crash : `default_tags` + sweeper (Resource Groups Tagging API / aws-nuke) ; teaser natif 1.16 `skip_cleanup`
*Slide/discussion, segment 4*
- State en mémoire, crash → buckets orphelins
- `default_tags` (providers.tf) pose un tag de run unique → `sweep.sh` les retrouve
- Resource Groups Tagging API (`aws resourcegroupstaggingapi get-resources --tag-filters`)
- Anecdote : SCP a forcé `eu-west-1` (garde-fou org réel)
- Teaser : `skip_cleanup` + `terraform test cleanup` en preview 1.16

#### **S3** : Reco layering (statique → terraform test → Terratest si e2e) + CTA
*Slide, segment 5*
- Layering : statique (tflint/trivy) → `terraform test` (unit mock + intégration) → Terratest si e2e/polling

### 5. TIPS ET PIÈGES (À COUVRIR EN LIVE)

**Source** : Pièce Live, section "Tips et pièges (à couvrir)"

#### **`variables {}` au niveau fichier**
- Pose des valeurs pour TOUS les `run {}` du fichier
- Chaque run surcharge au besoin
- Moins de répétition

#### **Runs unitaires pour les validations**
- Un validateur = un `run` (plan-only)
- Relecture simple, coût quasi nul
- Message d'erreur isolé

#### **Variables simples plutôt qu'objets complexes (retex)**
- `object({...})` rend le module visuellement propre mais complique l'usage et l'isolation du message d'erreur
- Pour ce qui doit être validé, préférer variables mono-type
- Un `validation` par attribut (plusieurs blocs par variable depuis 1.9)
- Continuité : live #1 portait `kms = object({arn, provided})` ; ici on aplatit pour la testabilité

#### **Wishlist HashiCorp (Fact-check Wave 1)**
- Pour une structure complexe, exposer l'attribut qui a déclenché l'erreur de validation
- Semble absent en 1.15.6, à confirmer sur le changelog 1.15/1.16

### 11. IMPLÉMENTATION RÉELLE DANS LE REPO (À COMPARER)

**Commit** : `f67c7e4 feat(terraform-module-testing): add native terraform test example`

#### Fichiers et structure implémentée

```
examples/terraform-module-testing/
├── .terraform-version          → 1.15.6
├── .terraform.lock.hcl
├── .tflint.hcl
├── Makefile                     → cibles test-fast, test-deploy, test-parallel, test
├── README.md                    → lien vers doc officielle, list des couches
├── AGENTS.md
├── providers.tf                 → default_tags avec tags tftest-suite/tftest-run
├── variables.tf                 → region, bucket_name, environment, enable_encryption, kms_key_arn, force_destroy, versioning_enabled, run_id
├── main.tf                      → module.bucket (root example)
├── outputs.tf                   → bucket_id, bucket_arn, versioning_status, encryption_enabled, kms_key_arn
├── sweep.sh                     → sweeper avec Resource Groups Tagging API
├── modules/s3-bucket/
│   ├── main.tf                  → bucket + public_access_block + versioning + SSE-KMS (count piloté sur kms_enabled)
│   ├── variables.tf             → bucket_name (validations regex), environment (enum), enable_encryption, kms_key_arn, force_destroy, versioning_enabled, tags
│   ├── outputs.tf
│   └── versions.tf              → Terraform >= 1.9.0, aws >= 5.0
└── tests/
    ├── setup/
    │   └── main.tf              → random_id pour suffixe unique
    ├── validations.tftest.hcl    → 5 runs avec expect_failures (bucket_name, environment, encryption_requires_kms_key, prod_forbids_force_destroy)
    ├── plan.tftest.hcl           → 4 runs avec mock_provider, assertions sur versioning/public-access/encryption
    ├── deploy.tftest.hcl         → 1 run apply réel (setup + deploy_and_assert)
    └── parallel.tftest.hcl       → 2 runs apply parallèles avec state_key distincts
```

#### Validations codées dans le module

**Dans `modules/s3-bucket/variables.tf`** :

1. **bucket_name** (L5-13) :
   - Longueur 3-63 caractères
   - Regex : `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9.-]*[a-z0-9]$`

2. **environment** (L16-24) :
   - Enum : `["dev", "staging", "prod"]`

3. **enable_encryption + kms_key_arn (croisée, L26-36)** :
   - `!var.enable_encryption || var.kms_key_arn != null`
   - Message : "kms_key_arn is required when enable_encryption is true."

4. **force_destroy + environment (croisée, L44-54)** :
   - `!(var.environment == "prod" && var.force_destroy)`
   - Message : "force_destroy must be false when environment is prod."

#### Tests implémentés

**`tests/validations.tftest.hcl`** (L1-70) :
- 5 runs atomiques avec `expect_failures` :
  - `bucket_name_too_short` : "ab" → expect fail on var.bucket_name
  - `bucket_name_invalid_chars` : "Mon_Bucket" → expect fail on var.bucket_name
  - `invalid_environment` : "production" → expect fail on var.environment
  - `encryption_requires_kms_key` : enable_encryption=true, kms_key_arn=null → expect fail on var.enable_encryption
  - `prod_forbids_force_destroy` : environment="prod", force_destroy=true → expect fail on var.force_destroy

**`tests/plan.tftest.hcl`** (L1-72) :
- `variables {}` au niveau fichier : bucket_name="demo-plan-bucket", environment="dev"
- 4 runs avec `mock_provider`, assertions sur config planifiée :
  - `versioning_enabled_by_default` : assert status == "Enabled"
  - `public_access_fully_blocked` : assert 4 flags = true
  - `no_encryption_block_without_key` : assert length(config) == 0
  - `encryption_block_present_with_key` : assert length(config) == 1 + ARN propagé

**`tests/deploy.tftest.hcl`** (L1-36) :
- Run `setup` : produit random_id.suffix (hex)
- Run `deploy_and_assert` : command=apply, variables bucket_name="bga-tftest-${run.setup.suffix}", assertions :
  - output.bucket_id == nom demandé
  - output.versioning_status == "Enabled"
  - output.encryption_enabled == false

**`tests/parallel.tftest.hcl`** (L1-42) :
- Run `setup` : random_id.suffix
- 2 runs apply parallèles :
  - `deploy_a` : parallel=true, state_key="a", bucket_name="bga-tftest-a-${run.setup.suffix}"
  - `deploy_b` : parallel=true, state_key="b", bucket_name="bga-tftest-b-${run.setup.suffix}"

#### Makefile targets

```makefile
make init              # terraform init (aws + random)
make fmt               # format check
make validate          # validate
make test-fast         # validations + plan (creds-free)
make test-deploy       # apply réel + assertions (AWS_PROFILE=sandbox)
make test-parallel     # parallel + state_key (AWS_PROFILE=sandbox)
make test              # suite complète
make sweep             # sweeper orphelins
make clean             # supprime .terraform + .tfstate*
```

#### Sweeper (sweep.sh)

- Cible Resource Groups Tagging API
- Tag-filter : `tftest-suite=terraform-module-testing`
- Usage : `./sweep.sh` (dry-run) ou `./sweep.sh --force` (exécute)

# Terraform `terraform test` Framework - Golden Pass Coverage Report

Cross-checked against context7 (`/websites/developer_hashicorp_terraform`) and official `developer.hashicorp.com` docs + `hashicorp/terraform` GitHub CHANGELOGs. Today = 2026-06; **1.15.0 is released, 1.16.0 is unreleased (experimental).**

## Version reality check
- The framework GA'd in **1.6.0** (Oct 2023). Mocking added in **1.7.0** (Feb 2024). Everything else is incremental through 1.11-1.16.
- The earlier web-search claim that "1.15 doesn't exist" is **stale** (model cutoff Jan 2026). 1.15.0 ships and 1.16.0 is the unreleased experimental line.

---

## 1. `.tftest.hcl` file structure

| Feature | What it does | Introduced | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Test files `.tftest.hcl` / `.tftest.json` | Discovered by extension; default dir `tests/` | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `run` block | Simulates a Terraform command in the config dir; sequential by default; 1..N per file | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `command = plan \| apply` | Selects operation; **default `apply`** | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| File-level `variables` block | 0..1; values passed to all runs | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| Run-level `variables` block | Overrides file-level for that run | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `module` block (run-level) | Loads an **alternate** module for the run; only `source` + `version` attrs | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `provider` blocks (file-level) | Configure providers like main config; **can reference test vars + prior run outputs** (`run.vault_setup.aws_access_key`) | 1.6.0 (refs 1.7.0+) | Stable |
| `providers` map (run-level) | Maps provider aliases → test-file providers | 1.6.0 | Stable |

## 2. Assertions & negative paths

| Feature | What it does | Introduced | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `assert { condition / error_message }` | Positive-path checks; failures reported at end | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `expect_failures = [ ... ]` | Negative-path: expects a checkable object to fail. Targets: **variables** (`var.x`), **outputs** (`output.y`), **resources**, **check blocks** | 1.6.0 | Stable |

Caveat (doc-confirmed): custom conditions other than `check` blocks **halt** the plan/apply. Don't mix `expect_failures` (on a variable) with `assert` in the same run expecting the assert to run - the halt skips the assert. Best practice: one or the other per run.

## 3. Mocking & overrides

| Feature | What it does | Introduced | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `mock_provider "x" {}` | Fake provider; auto-generates synthetic computed values, no creds/API | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `mock_resource { type, defaults }` (nested in `mock_provider`) | Override resource computed values; provider not called | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `mock_data { type, defaults }` | Same for data sources | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `defaults` object | Per-type returned attribute values inside mock/override | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `override_resource { target, values }` | Replace specific resource values (file or run level) | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `override_data { target, values }` | Replace data-source values | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `override_module { target, outputs }` | Replace a module's outputs (uses `outputs`, not `values`) | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `*.tfmock.hcl` / `.tfmock.json` files | Reusable `mock_resource`/`mock_data` libraries, loaded via `source` in `mock_provider` | 1.7.0 | Stable |
| `override_during = plan \| apply` | When mocked/overridden values materialize. **Default `apply`** (returns `(known after apply)` at plan). Set `plan` to use values during plan-only unit runs | **1.11.0** (#36227) | Stable |
| Functions inside mock blocks | `defaults` may call Terraform functions | **1.15.0** (#34672) | Stable |

Auto-generated value rules: number→0, bool→false, string→random 8-char alnum, collections→empty, objects→recursive.

## 4. Parallelism

| Feature | What it does | Introduced | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `parallel = true` (run/test level) | Run independent run blocks concurrently | **1.12.0** (#34237) | Stable |
| `-parallelism=<n>` CLI | Concurrent plan/apply ops within a run; **default 10** | **1.12.0** | Stable |
| `state_key` (run block) | Controls which internal state file a run uses; lets unrelated runs share state | **1.11.0** (#36185) | Stable |
| Parallel teardown | Teardown ops run in parallel | **1.13.0** (#37169) | Stable |
| Parallelism=1 deadlock fix | - | 1.13.0 (#37292) | Stable |

Two runs go parallel only if: both `parallel=true`, no output dependency, and different state keys (no shared state). Note your earlier guess (1.12/1.13): **`state_key` = 1.11**, **`parallel`/`-parallelism` = 1.12**.

## 5. Run command & plan_options

| Feature | What it does | Introduced | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `plan_options { mode }` | `normal` (default) or `refresh-only` | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `plan_options { refresh }` | bool, default `true` | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `plan_options { replace = [...] }` | Force-replace resource addresses | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `plan_options { target = [...] }` | Limit to resource addresses | 1.6.0 | Stable |

## 6. Setup/helper modules & run chaining

| Feature | What it does | Introduced | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Setup/helper module pattern | `tests/setup/` modules build prerequisites (e.g. random names); separate from module under test | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `run.<name>.<output>` references | Later runs consume prior run outputs (e.g. `run.setup_tests.bucket_prefix`) | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| File-level vars referencing run outputs/other vars | File-level `variables` can reference run outputs and other variables | **1.13.0** (#37205) | Stable |
| External variable definitions in test files | `variable` blocks defining externally-referenced vars; **now required** when referenced | **1.13.0** (#37195, upgrade note) | Stable |

## 8. `terraform test` CLI

| Flag | What it does | Introduced | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `-filter=<file>` | Limit to specified test file(s) | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `-verbose` | Print plan/state per run; (1.14: includes expected diagnostics) | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `-json` | Machine-readable output | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `-test-directory=<dir>` | Override `tests/` dir | 1.6.0 | Stable |
| `-no-cleanup` | Skip resource destroy after run (confirmed via docs/search) | 1.6.x | Stable |
| `-junit-xml=<path>` | JUnit XML report (incompatible with `-cloud-run`) | **GA in 1.11.0** (#36324) | Stable |
| `-parallelism=<n>` | See §4 | 1.12.0 | Stable |
| `-cloud-run=<module source>` | Run remotely on HCP Terraform | 1.6.x | Stable |
| JUnit skipped-test diagnostics | File-level errors surfaced in JUnit `skipped` elements for CI | **1.15.0** (#37801) | Stable |

## 9. Notable changelog entries by version

- **1.10** - no headline `test` changes (incremental fixes).
- **1.11.0** - `-junit-xml` **GA** (#36324); `override_during = plan` (#36227); **`state_key`** (#36185).
- **1.12.0** - `parallel` annotation + `-parallelism=n` (#34237); continue execution when expected failure not encountered; detailed diagnostic objects on assert failure; `init` allowed when only tests present.
- **1.13.0** (Aug 20 2025) - parallel teardown (#37169); external variable definitions in test files (#37195, **breaking upgrade note**); file-level var blocks reference run outputs/vars (#37205); parallelism=1 deadlock fix.
- **1.14.0** (Nov 19 2025) - expected diagnostics in verbose output (#37362); **ignore `prevent_destroy` during test cleanup** (#37364); console/test explicit diagnostics for resources absent from last op (#37663).
- **1.15.0** - **functions allowed in mock blocks** (#34672); JUnit XML skipped-element diagnostics (#37801); `.tfquery.hcl` fmt support (#38398, query feature, not test).
- **1.16.0 (UNRELEASED / EXPERIMENTAL)**:
  - **`terraform test cleanup`** command - cleans up state files left by interrupted/failed test runs; a manifest + state files for failed cleanups saved in `.terraform/`.
  - **`skip_cleanup`** attribute (test-file and run level) - tells `terraform test` not to destroy/clean state for that run; left in `.terraform/` for manual `test cleanup`.
  - **`backend` blocks inside `run` blocks** - load state from a backend instead of empty state each run; keeps long-running test infra alive between operations.
  - ENHANCEMENT: "Show info when resources are left behind due to `skip_cleanup`."

## 10. Registry / HCP Terraform

- `-cloud-run=app.terraform.io/ORG/module/provider` runs tests **remotely on HCP Terraform** against a **private registry module**; centralizes creds, integrates CI/CD for published modules. Incompatible with `-junit-xml`.

## 11. HashiCorp best practices (tutorial)

- Tests live in `tests/`; helper/setup modules in `tests/setup/`.
- Module repo naming `terraform-<PROVIDER>-<NAME>`; descriptive run-block names.
- `command = plan` for cheap logic/validation checks; `command = apply` to assert real created state.
- Tests act as combined unit + integration; guard against breaking changes.
- Mock expensive resources (EC2/RDS) with `override_resource`/`mock_provider` to speed runs.
- Chain runs via `run.<name>.<output>`; teardown is reverse run order.

---

# GAP CHECKLIST (tick what your demo covers)

**MUST-SHOW GOLDEN (core of a 20-min module-testing demo):**
- [ ] `.tftest.hcl` in `tests/`, `terraform test` invocation
- [ ] `run` block with `command = plan` vs `command = apply`
- [ ] `assert { condition / error_message }` (positive path)
- [ ] File-level vs run-level `variables` (override precedence)
- [ ] `expect_failures = [var.x]` for **variable validation** (negative path) - the canonical "even/odd" example
- [ ] `mock_provider "aws" {}` - run tests with zero cloud cost/creds (THE headline feature)
- [ ] `override_resource` / `override_data` to skip slow resources (EC2/RDS)
- [ ] Setup/helper module + `run.<setup>.<output>` chaining (random bucket name pattern)
- [ ] `-verbose` to show plan/state per run

**STRONG SECONDARY (show if time / "gold" polish):**
- [ ] `mock_resource` / `mock_data` with `defaults` inside `mock_provider`
- [ ] `*.tfmock.hcl` reusable mock library via `source`
- [ ] `override_during = plan` (1.11) - mocked values during plan-only unit runs
- [ ] `plan_options { mode = refresh-only / refresh / replace / target }`
- [ ] `-junit-xml` report for CI (1.11 GA)
- [ ] `expect_failures` against outputs/resources/checks (beyond variables)

**NICHE / ADVANCED (mention, don't dwell):**
- [ ] `parallel = true` + `-parallelism=n` (1.12) and `state_key` (1.11)
- [ ] `module` block alternate-module loading
- [ ] `providers` alias mapping; provider config referencing run outputs
- [ ] Functions inside mock `defaults` (1.15)
- [ ] `-cloud-run` against HCP Terraform / Registry modules
- [ ] External `variable` definitions requirement (1.13 breaking change - worth a caveat slide)

**BLEEDING EDGE / PREVIEW (flag as "coming in 1.16, experimental"):**
- [ ] `terraform test cleanup` command
- [ ] `skip_cleanup` attribute
- [ ] `backend` blocks inside `run` blocks (persistent test state)

## Demo gotcha to teach
Don't combine `expect_failures` on a variable with an `assert` in the same run - the variable validation halts the plan and the assert never evaluates. One pattern per run.

## Key sources
- [Tests - Configuration Language](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/tests)
- [Mocking](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/tests/mocking)
- [terraform test CLI](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/test)

- Mock expensive resources (EC2/RDS) with `override_resource`/`mock_provider` to speed runs.
- Chain runs via `run.<name>.<output>`; teardown is reverse run order.

---

# GAP CHECKLIST (tick what your demo covers)

**MUST-SHOW GOLDEN (core of a 20-min module-testing demo):**
- [ ] `.tftest.hcl` in `tests/`, `terraform test` invocation
- [ ] `run` block with `command = plan` vs `command = apply`
- [ ] `assert { condition / error_message }` (positive path)
- [ ] File-level vs run-level `variables` (override precedence)
- [ ] `expect_failures = [var.x]` for **variable validation** (negative path) - the canonical "even/odd" example
- [ ] `mock_provider "aws" {}` - run tests with zero cloud cost/creds (THE headline feature)
- [ ] `override_resource` / `override_data` to skip slow resources (EC2/RDS)
- [ ] Setup/helper module + `run.<setup>.<output>` chaining (random bucket name pattern)
- [ ] `-verbose` to show plan/state per run

**STRONG SECONDARY (show if time / "gold" polish):**
- [ ] `mock_resource` / `mock_data` with `defaults` inside `mock_provider`
- [ ] `*.tfmock.hcl` reusable mock library via `source`
- [ ] `override_during = plan` (1.11) - mocked values during plan-only unit runs
- [ ] `plan_options { mode = refresh-only / refresh / replace / target }`
- [ ] `-junit-xml` report for CI (1.11 GA)
- [ ] `expect_failures` against outputs/resources/checks (beyond variables)

**NICHE / ADVANCED (mention, don't dwell):**
- [ ] `parallel = true` + `-parallelism=n` (1.12) and `state_key` (1.11)
- [ ] `module` block alternate-module loading
- [ ] `providers` alias mapping; provider config referencing run outputs
- [ ] Functions inside mock `defaults` (1.15)
- [ ] `-cloud-run` against HCP Terraform / Registry modules
- [ ] External `variable` definitions requirement (1.13 breaking change - worth a caveat slide)

**BLEEDING EDGE / PREVIEW (flag as "coming in 1.16, experimental"):**
- [ ] `terraform test cleanup` command
- [ ] `skip_cleanup` attribute
- [ ] `backend` blocks inside `run` blocks (persistent test state)

## Demo gotcha to teach
Don't combine `expect_failures` on a variable with an `assert` in the same run - the variable validation halts the plan and the assert never evaluates. One pattern per run.

## Key sources
- [Tests - Configuration Language](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/tests)
- [Mocking](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/tests/mocking)
- [terraform test CLI](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/test)
- [Write Terraform Tests tutorial](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/tutorials/configuration-language/test)
- CHANGELOGs: [1.11.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.11.0/CHANGELOG.md), [1.12.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.12.0/CHANGELOG.md), [1.13.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.13.0/CHANGELOG.md), [1.14.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.14.0/CHANGELOG.md), [1.15.0](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/v1.15.0/CHANGELOG.md), [main/1.16 unreleased](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)

One correction to flag for your lab notes: **`state_key` is 1.11, not 1.12/1.13**, and **`parallel`/`-parallelism` is 1.12**. Mocking itself is 1.7 (not a 1.1x feature). 1.16's cleanup/skip_cleanup/backend-in-run trio is still **experimental and unreleased** as of now - demo it only on an experimental build with a caveat.
CI config lue. Une question clé pour le plan: la couche creds-free tourne-t-elle réellement en CI? Je vérifie le workflow terraform.

name: Terraform quality

on:
  push:
    branches: ['**']
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: terraform-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  discover:
    name: Discover Terraform root modules
    # Always run on push; on pull_request, run only for fork PRs
    # (same-repo PRs are already covered by the push event).
    if: github.event_name == 'push' || github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name != github.repository
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      cells: ${{ steps.find.outputs.cells }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - id: find
        # Any directory under examples/ containing providers.tf is a root module to validate.
        # name = path minus examples/ ; example = examples/<name> (holds the per-example tooling).
        run: |
          cells=$(find examples -name providers.tf -printf '%h\n' | sort \
            | jq -R -s '
                split("\n")
                | map(select(length > 0))
                | map({
                    dir: .,
                    name: (sub("^examples/"; "")),
                    example: (split("/")[0:2] | join("/"))
                  })')
          echo "cells=$(echo "$cells" | jq -c .)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  fmt:
    name: terraform fmt
    needs: discover
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/tf-setup
        with:
          install-tflint: 'false'
      - run: terraform fmt -check -recursive -diff

  quality:
    name: validate + lint + scan (${{ matrix.cell.name }})
    needs: discover
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        cell: ${{ fromJSON(needs.discover.outputs.cells) }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3

      - uses: ./.github/actions/tf-setup
        with:
          working-directory: ${{ matrix.cell.dir }}

      - name: terraform init (no backend)
        working-directory: ${{ matrix.cell.dir }}
        run: terraform init -backend=false -input=false

      - name: terraform validate
        working-directory: ${{ matrix.cell.dir }}
        run: terraform validate -no-color

      - name: tflint init
        working-directory: ${{ matrix.cell.dir }}
        run: tflint --init --config "${{ github.workspace }}/${{ matrix.cell.example }}/.tflint.hcl"
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: tflint
        working-directory: ${{ matrix.cell.dir }}
        run: tflint --recursive --format compact --config "${{ github.workspace }}/${{ matrix.cell.example }}/.tflint.hcl"

      - name: install trivy
        uses: aquasecurity/setup-trivy@81e514348e19b6112ce2a7e3ecbafe19c1e1f567 # v0.3.1
        with:
          # Pinned for reproducible CI and bumped by Renovate (annotation below).
          # v0.71.1 once failed to install via setup-trivy's get.trivy.dev path, so
          # CI gates any bump - keep an eye on the Renovate PR's checks.
          # renovate: datasource=github-releases depName=aquasecurity/trivy
          version: v0.71.0
          cache: true

      - name: trivy config scan (report + gate)
        working-directory: ${{ matrix.cell.dir }}
        run: |
          # Scan once to JSON, then convert the same results twice:
          #   - report: MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL, never fails the job (visibility).
          #   - gate:   HIGH,CRITICAL, fails the job (exit-code 1) if any are found.
          trivy config . --format json --output trivy.json --exit-code 0

          echo "::group::Trivy findings (MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL - report only)"
          trivy convert --format table --severity MEDIUM,HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 0 trivy.json
          echo "::endgroup::"

          trivy convert --format table --severity HIGH,CRITICAL --exit-code 1 trivy.json

content.text:
Analyse complète. Synthèse ci-dessous, puis la question de priorisation.

⚠️ **Contrainte temporelle**: live demain (2026-06-25, J-1). Code + slides déjà faits (PR #33). Tout ce qui suit est polish/amélioration, pas du blocage.

## 1. Prévu (conception éditoriale) vs Présent (repo)

| # | Élément prévu (pièce/idée) | Type | Présent dans le repo ? |
|---|---|---|---|
| L1 | Module S3 child + exemple root | live-code | ✅ `modules/s3-bucket/` + `main.tf` |
| L2 | Validation simple + `expect_failures` rouge→vert | live-code | 🟡 Tests ✅ ; **geste rouge→vert non documenté** dans le repo |
| L3 | Validations croisées (1.9+) chiffrement⇒KMS, prod⇒¬force_destroy | live-code | ✅ `variables.tf` + 2 runs |
| L4 | KMS conditionnel `count` (continuité deferred actions) | live-code | ✅ `main.tf` locals |
| L5 | Test plan + `mock_provider`, creds-free | live-code | ✅ `plan.tftest.hcl` |
| L6 | Apply réel sandbox + assertions + auto-destroy | live-code | ✅ `deploy.tftest.hcl` |
| L7 | `parallel` + `state_key`, 2 déploiements | live-code | ✅ `parallel.tftest.hcl` |
| S1 | **CI gate : couche mock tourne sans secret** | slide | ❌ **README l'affirme, mais `terraform.yml` ne lance PAS `terraform test`** |
| S2 | Cleanup crash : `default_tags` + sweeper + teaser 1.16 | slide | ✅ `sweep.sh` + `providers.tf` + README |
| I1-I4, S3 | Slides (pyramide, comparatif outils, layering, CTA) | slide | ➖ Assets éditoriaux (hors repo, normal) |
| - | Structure **démo progressive en stages** (pyramide→stages) | conception | 🟡 Makefile a les cibles, **README est une référence plate, pas un walkthrough staged** |

**2 vrais gaps**: S1 (CI ne joue pas les tests creds-free, contredit le README + la slide) et la **structure progressive** (le README ne raconte pas les étapes de la pyramide).

## 2. Golden pass `terraform test` (couverture vs doc 1.6→1.16)

Couvert (golden core): `.tftest.hcl`, `run`/`command plan|apply`, `assert`, `expect_failures` (variables), `mock_provider`, setup module + `run.x.output`, file-level vs run-level `variables`, `parallel`+`state_key`. **Très bonne base.**

Manquant, par valeur pour ce live:
- 🥇 `-junit-xml` (GA 1.11) — **rapport CI**, sert directement la narrative S1 "CI gate"
- 🥇 Câbler la couche creds-free dans la CI (ferme le gap S1)
- 🥈 `override_resource`/`mock_resource { defaults }` — profondeur du mocking au-delà du `mock_provider {}` nu
- 🥈 **Functions in mock `defaults` (nouveauté 1.15)** — un highlight "version récente" gratuit
- 🥉 `override_during = plan` (1.11), `plan_options` (refresh-only/replace/target), `*.tfmock.hcl`, `expect_failures` sur outputs/checks — niche
- 🔮 1.16 preview `skip_cleanup` / `test cleanup` — déjà teasé au README ✅

Correction doc à porter: commentaire `parallel.tftest.hcl:2` dit `state_key (>= 1.12)` — en fait **`state_key` = 1.11**, `parallel` = 1.12.

## 3. Cohérence langue — violation confirmée

Tout l'exemple est en **FR**, le reste du repo en **EN** (root AGENTS.md, README, autres exemples). À traduire: `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, commentaires de `Makefile` + 4 `.tftest.hcl` + `main.tf`/`variables.tf`/`providers.tf`/`setup/main.tf`, et commentaires **+ strings d'output FR** de `sweep.sh`. Les identifiants sont déjà EN.

**Cause racine**: aucun AGENTS.md ne stipule explicitement "anglais partout" — la convention est seulement implicite par mimétisme. D'où le dérapage.

## 4. Plan de mise à jour AGENTS.md (conventions)

| Convention | État actuel | Proposition |
|---|---|---|
| **Langue EN partout** (code/commentaires/docs) | implicite seulement → a laissé passer le FR | **Ajouter une règle explicite** au root AGENTS.md (anti-récurrence #1) |
| Conventional Commits | ✅ documenté | Mentionner les gates `commitlint.yml` + `pr-title.yml` (semantic PR title) |
| Semantic release | ✅ release-please documenté | RAS |
| CI lint (fmt/tflint/trivy) | ✅ documenté | RAS |
| **Pre-commit** | ❌ absent | **Proposer** `.pre-commit-config.yaml` (fmt/tflint/validate locaux, miroir CI) — process à mettre en place |
| `terraform test` en CI | ❌ non câblé | Documenter une fois câblé (cf. gap S1) |

## 5. Chantiers de travail

- **A — Démo progressive** : réécrire le README en stages mappés à la pyramide (Stage 0 présenter module/structure → 1 validations → 2 plan/mock creds-free → 3 deploy intégration → 4 parallel), chaque stage = couche + cible make + ce qu'il prouve + geste rouge→vert documenté. Cibles Makefile atomiques par stage si besoin.
- **B — Langue FR→EN** : traduire les 11 fichiers ci-dessus.
- **C — AGENTS.md conventions** : règle langue EN + gates commit + (décision) pre-commit.
- **D — Golden features** : câbler couche creds-free + `-junit-xml` en CI (ferme S1), mini-démo `override_resource`, highlight functions-in-mock 1.15, fix versions.

# Demo run-of-show - live "Terraform test" (2026-06-25).
# terraform 1.15.6 (see .terraform-version). The real apply targets the sandbox AWS profile.
# Targets are ordered as the progressive demo stages (read the README "Progressive demo").
TF := terraform

.PHONY: init fmt validate test-validate test-plan test-fast test-deploy test-parallel test sweep clean

init:           ## init (providers: aws + random)
	$(TF) init

fmt:            ## format check
	$(TF) fmt -recursive -check -diff

validate:       ## validate the config
	$(TF) validate

test-validate:  ## Stage 1 - variable validations only (expect_failures, creds-free)
	$(TF) test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -verbose

test-plan:      ## Stage 2 - mocked plan assertions only (mock_provider, creds-free)
	$(TF) test -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl -verbose

test-fast:      ## Stages 1+2 - creds-free layer (runs in CI without secrets)
	$(TF) test -filter=tests/validations.tftest.hcl -filter=tests/plan.tftest.hcl -verbose

test-deploy:    ## Stage 3 - integration: real apply on AWS (requires AWS_PROFILE=sandbox)
	AWS_PROFILE=sandbox $(TF) test -filter=tests/deploy.tftest.hcl

test-parallel:  ## Stage 4 - parallel + state_key: 2 concurrent deployments (AWS_PROFILE=sandbox)
	AWS_PROFILE=sandbox $(TF) test -filter=tests/parallel.tftest.hcl

test:           ## full suite (AWS_PROFILE=sandbox for the apply stages)
	AWS_PROFILE=sandbox $(TF) test

sweep:          ## clean up orphaned resources by tag (see sweep.sh)
	./sweep.sh

clean:          ## remove local terraform state
	find . -type d -name '.terraform' -prune -exec rm -rf {} + ; find . -name '*.tfstate*' -delete
